title: Public willingness to receive the AstraZeneca COVID-19 vaccine in Germany during the first half of 2021

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Based on early COVIMO baseline trends for AZ acceptance
Estimated: Reflects negative media coverage of side effects
Estimated: Post-suspension of AZ rollout in Germany
Estimated: Recovery following EMA safety reassurance
Estimated: Gradual normalization of vaccine perception
Estimated: Lifting of priority groups for AZ

events.description: Austrian authorities reported the death of a 49-year-old woman and the hospitalization of a 35-year-old following vaccination, both linked to a specific batch of the AstraZeneca vaccine.
Denmark, Norway, and Iceland suspended the use of the AstraZeneca vaccine as a precautionary measure following reports of serious blood clot cases.
Major European nations, including Germany, France, and Italy, paused the rollout of the AstraZeneca vaccine to await further investigation by the European Medicines Agency.
The European Medicines Agency (EMA) concluded that the vaccine is safe and effective, stating the benefits outweigh the risks, leading several countries to resume vaccinations.
The EMA announced that unusual blood clots with low blood platelets should be listed as very rare side effects of the AstraZeneca vaccine.
Following the EMA's update, the UK's Joint Committee on Vaccination and Immunisation recommended that adults under 30 be offered alternative vaccines where available.

agents.name: Emer Cooke
agents._role: Executive Director of the European Medicines Agency (EMA)

agents.name: Robert Koch Institute (RKI)
agents.description: Primary public health authority in Germany

agents.name: Skeptical Citizen Cluster
agents.description: Population segment concerned about vaccine side effects

agents.name: Pro-Vaccination Citizen Cluster
agents.description: Population segment eager for any available vaccine

notes: Pro-side defined as individuals willing to receive the AstraZeneca vaccine. Against-side defined as individuals unwilling or hesitant to receive the AstraZeneca vaccine. Data quality: Early rounds are estimates based on COVIMO trend reports; final round aligns with official RKI survey data.
